channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Small deep hole, 7th May
item.description:
There is a small very deep hole next to the drain on the road. It looks like it goes as far down as the whole drain and has started to spread outwards. It’s right outside number 86 driveway.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Nightingale Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bin bag dumped on street, 7th May
item.description:
One black bin bag dumped
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, North Kensington, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Cricket Nets, 7th May
item.description:
These cricket nets are a valuable asset to children practicing the game but they continue to be poorly maintained and misused. Please can the council’s contractor address the following: 1.On the right hand net the upper part needs a few zip tags so it doesn’t drop. 2. Throughout the nets have holes and do not serve their purpose 3. Clear signage required that these a cricket nets and not for dog training or anything other than cricket 4. The gate to the nets needs to work so it can be safely closed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Recreation Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Sainsbury shopping trolley, 7th May
item.description:
Trolley has been there over 24hrs

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Meadowcourt Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped Children’s Car Seat, 7th May
item.description:
Irresponsible dumping of children’s car seat by the entrance to Mayow Par

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Recreation Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 7th May
item.description:
Far too much graffiti in the area at present

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Murchison Avenue, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 7th May
item.description:
Flytipping in car parking bays opposite Marjorie McClure
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hawkwood Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 7th May
item.description:
This vehicle has been parked/abandoned outside my property for at least 10yrs,although this car is registered as classic & probably tax exempt & legally parked & checked by the police, it has never had any attempt to maintain it in anyway, I have never seen anyone (the owner) take any interest in fixing it. Over the years it is slowly but consistently being vandalised by children/teenagers who jump on the roof & bonnet,climb inside it & drink alcohol & smoke drugs & smash it up. I also think rats may be nesting in it . This abandoned vehicle is a health & safety issue & I’m concerned that it won’t be too long before it’s set on fire. Surely this should be a concern to Greenwich council. If it is abandoned can it be removed ? or can you contact the owner to take responsibility of this problem by removing from the location it’s been dumped at.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Meridian Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress dumped, 7th May
item.description:
Mattress dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hollydale Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Racist and offensive by stream, 7th May
item.description:
Racist and offencive graffiti very visible to public and children.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bladindon Drive, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fast food box + plastic bag on the street, 7th May
item.description:
On Leroy street in front of Stewart house ( flat 9 to 16), is constant victim of men eating fast food in their cars and littering it on the pavement ( see picture attached). It became urgent to install video surveillance and track those individuals. I trust you deal With it ASAP. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leroy Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A lid is loose, this could be dangerous, 7th May
item.description:
Small lid broken, I stepped on it and almost tripped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prince George Road, Shacklewell, Greater London

item.title: Dead baby fox, 7th May

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sparrows Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No waste collection at No 10, 7th May
item.description:
This is the 3rd time we have this issue, where the collectors are not removing our rubbish on the scheduled day. There is no information from Hackney indicating that tenants have to place bags on pavement for removal, today was bin day and No# 10 was left

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Foulden Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flat 28 wilton house repeat flytipper, 7th May
item.description:
Flat 28 Wilton house has repeatedly thrown their unwanted item out in the form of flytipping rather than contacting the council and paying for the removal. They dumped it in the passage outside the flat then left it on the stairwell, now have dumped it at the bottom of the stairs. This is flytipping and makes the area a mess. Flat 28 wilton house SE228AE.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albrighton Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
